question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: readmitDelay field / readmitTime AtomicReference] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition in tryReadmit() where the readmitTime is checked and then active is set to true non-atomically, allowing a concurrent error to set active=false and update readmitTime after the check but before the assignment, causing the node to be incorrectly readmitted and then immediately fail, creating an inconsistent state that prevents stable block ingestion and leaves token freeze states unprocessed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user, by operating a block node that sends a stream response of size exactly maxStreamResponseSize+1 bytes, cause the gRPC channel to throw a StatusRuntimeException with RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, which is caught by catch(Exception ex) in streamBlocks(), calls onError(), and after maxSubscribeAttempts failures deactivates the node, halting ingestion of token freeze/unfreeze transactions and permanently freezing the mirror node's token state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user, by operating a block node that sends a SubscribeStreamResponse.Code.NOT_AVAILABLE status (not SUCCESS), trigger the BlockStreamException 'Received status NOT_AVAILABLE from block node' in streamBlocks(), which calls onError(), and by repeating this exactly maxSubscribeAttempts=3 times, deactivate the node, and if this is the only configured block node, permanently halt block ingestion and freeze the mirror node's token account states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user, by exploiting the fact that responseTimeout=400ms is the minimum allowed value (@DurationMin(millis=100)), configure a deployment where responseTimeout is set to 100ms (minimum), and then introduce 101ms of latency to the block node's ServerStatus endpoint, causing getBlockRange() to always time out and return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE, making all nodes appear to have no blocks and permanently halting token transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxBlockItems field / BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a block node send an END_OF_BLOCK message before any BLOCK_HEADER has been received (pending.isEmpty() == true), causing Utility.handleRecoverableError() to be called in onEndOfBlock(), and if handleRecoverableError() does not throw but instead logs and continues, allow the assembler to remain in an inconsistent state where subsequent blocks are processed incorrectly, potentially causing token freeze transactions to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: readmitDelay field] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing all block nodes to be deactivated and then exploiting the fact that readmitDelay has a minimum of 10 seconds, ensure that during the readmit window, any token unfreeze transactions that were included in blocks that the mirror node missed are permanently lost (not re-fetched), causing the mirror node to permanently report those accounts as frozen even after the on-chain state has been corrected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize is a DataSize validated only as @NotNull with no maximum bound, and by injecting a configuration value of Long.MAX_VALUE bytes, cause the (int) cast in BlockNode constructor to overflow to a negative value, making gRPC reject all inbound messages, triggering repeated onError() calls, deactivating all block nodes, and permanently halting token state ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field / errors.set(0)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that errors.set(0) is called inside the while loop in streamBlocks() after each successful response (not after a complete block), so that a block node that alternates between sending valid responses and triggering errors can keep the error counter from reaching maxSubscribeAttempts, maintaining a degraded state where blocks are partially processed and token freeze transactions may be silently skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user, by operating a block node that responds to ServerStatus requests within responseTimeout=400ms but returns a block range that does not include the required block number, cause BlockNodeSubscriber.hasBlock() to return false, and by doing this for all configured block nodes, force BlockNodeSubscriber.getNode() to throw BlockStreamException('No block node can provide block N'), permanently halting block ingestion and freezing the mirror node's token state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a block node send a block where pendingCount is exactly maxBlockItems=800000 (at the limit), verify that no exception is thrown (since the check is > not >=), and then send a follow-up block with pendingCount=maxBlockItems+1, triggering BlockStreamException, calling onError(), and after maxSubscribeAttempts exhaustion deactivating the node and halting token transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: readmitDelay field / tryReadmit()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that tryReadmit(true) (force=true) is called in BlockNodeSubscriber.getNode() when no active node has the required block, allowing a deactivated node to be force-readmitted and immediately fail again, and by ensuring the force-readmitted node always fails, create an infinite loop of deactivation and force-readmission that prevents stable block ingestion and leaves token freeze states permanently unprocessed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user, by operating a block node that sends responses just under maxStreamResponseSize=36MB but containing token freeze transactions, and then suddenly sending a response just over 36MB containing a critical token unfreeze transaction, cause that specific unfreeze transaction to be rejected by the gRPC size limit, triggering onError() and node deactivation, while the freeze transaction was already processed, resulting in a permanently frozen token account state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user, by operating a block node that sends a gRPC stream that abruptly closes (simulating a network disconnect) without sending a SUCCESS status, trigger the null return from grpcCall.read() in streamBlocks(), which exits the while loop without calling onError(), and by exploiting this to avoid incrementing the error counter, maintain a node in an active but non-functional state that never processes token freeze/unfreeze transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user, by exploiting the fact that responseTimeout is used with withDeadlineAfter() in getBlockRange() but the deadline is applied to the entire RPC call including connection establishment, cause legitimate block nodes behind slow networks to consistently time out, while an attacker-controlled node responds quickly but provides incorrect block ranges, making the attacker's node the only one selected and allowing manipulation of which token transactions are processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: maxBlockItems field / BlockAssembler.onEndOfBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a block node send an END_OF_BLOCK with a block number that mismatches the BLOCK_HEADER number, causing Utility.handleRecoverableError() to be called in onEndOfBlock(), and if handleRecoverableError() does not throw, allow the block to be assembled and passed to blockStreamConsumer with the wrong block number, potentially causing token freeze/unfreeze transactions to be associated with the wrong block and processed out of order?
